name: MITRE.LIN.T1190.RULE Exploit Public-Facing Application
notes:
No action required
Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:MITRE.LIN.T1190: Public-Facing Application
when the source is vulnerable to any exploit on any port
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1190.RULE Exploit Public-Facing Application on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:MITRE.LIN.T1190: Public-Facing Application
AND when the source is vulnerable to any exploit on any port
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1190.RULE Exploit Public-Facing Application

Event description: The use of software, data, or commands to take advantage of a weakness in an Internet-facing computer system or program in order to cause unintended or unanticipated behavior. The weakness in the system can be a bug, a glitch, or a design vulnerability.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
